001import java.util.StringTokenizer;
002
003/**
004 * This class is part of the "World of Zuul" application.
005 * "World of Zuul" is a very simple, text based adventure game.
006 *
007 * This parser receives a line of user input as a String and tries to
008 *  * interpret it as an adventure command. It returns the command as an
009 *  * object of class Command.
010 *
011 * The parser has a set of known command words. It checks user input against
012 * the known commands, and if the input is not one of the known commands, it
013 * returns a command object that is marked as an unknown command.
014 *
015 * @author  Michael Kolling and David J. Barnes + D.Bureau + Raphaël Quillaud
016 * @version 2008.03.30 + 2013.09.15 + 2026.05.09
017 */
018public class Parser {
019    private CommandWords aValidCW;  // (see CommandWords class)
020
021    /**
022     * Default constructor that creates the CommandWords object.
023     */
024    public Parser() {
025        this.aValidCW = new CommandWords();
026    } // Parser()
027
028    /**
029     * Reads the next command from the user.
030     *
031     * @param pInputLine the line entered by the user
032     * @return the corresponding command
033     */
034    public Command getCommand(final String pInputLine) {
035        String vWord1 = null;
036        String vWord2 = null;
037
038        StringTokenizer vTokenizer = new StringTokenizer(pInputLine);
039
040        if (vTokenizer.hasMoreTokens()) {
041            vWord1 = vTokenizer.nextToken();
042        }
043        if (vTokenizer.hasMoreTokens()) {
044            vWord2 = vTokenizer.nextToken();
045        }
046
047        if (this.aValidCW.isCommand(vWord1)) {
048            return new Command(vWord1, vWord2);
049        }
050        else {
051            return new Command(null, vWord2);
052        }
053    } // getCommand()
054
055    /**
056     * Gives access to the set of valid command words.
057     *
058     * @return the CommandWords instance used by this parser
059     */
060    public CommandWords getCommandWords() {
061        return this.aValidCW;
062    }
063
064    /**
065     * Returns the list of all valid command words as a single string.
066     * This method simply forwards the request to the {@link CommandWords}
067     * object used by the parser.
068     *
069     * @return a space-separated list of all valid commands
070     */
071    public String getCommandList() {
072        return this.aValidCW.getCommandList();
073    }
074} // Parser
